title: miliusa sessilis
intro: Miliusa sessilis, also known as the Pahit, is a small plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Miliusa sessilis include its ability to treat fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Miliusa sessilis is often used as a decoction, made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or as a tea, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
